name: Grilled Salmon with Potato and Watercress Salad
recipeIngredient: About 0.5 pound watercress, rinsed and crisped
2 cups Alder or mesquite wood chips, soaked in water
recipeInstructions:
1. In a 5-to 6-quart pan, bring about 2 quarts of water to a boil over high heat.
2. Add potatoes and simmer over low heat until tender when pierced (15-20 minutes).
3. Drain the potatoes and chill.
4. Soak onions in cold water that covers them for about 15 minutes; then drain.
5. Mix drained onions with seasoned rice vinegar.
6. Cut potatoes into quarters and add to onions.
7. Trim tender stems from watercress, finely chop enough course stems to make 0.5 cup (discard extras or save).
8. Mix chopped stems on a large oval platter with potato salad; cover and keep cool.
9. Rinse salmon and pat dry. Place skin side down on heavy foil, cutting around the fish leaving a 1-inch border.
10. Crimp edges of foil to fit tightly against the edge of fish.
11. Mix soy sauce with brown sugar and brush onto salmon fillet.
12. Lay fish in center of grill ensuring it is not over coals or flame. Cover barbecue (open vents for charcoal) and cook until barely opaque in thickest part when tested by cutting, 15-20 minutes.
13. Transfer fish to platter with salad, add salt to taste. Serve hot or cold.
